plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI and QQQ exposure to 25% and 20% respectively, focusing on income and broad index stability.
Reduce SMH to 5% due to its downtrend, further de-risking the portfolio.
Hold 15% cash as a buffer for capital preservation and future opportunities.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk, particularly from remaining equity exposure.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to reduce exposure to highly leveraged ETFs.
Trim QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for trimming, reallocating capital.
Increase QQQ to 25% for balanced broad market exposure.
Buy TXN at 10% as a high-scoring, quality tech setup from the scanner.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer to provide flexibility and manage risk.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth by reducing leverage and diversifying. It still carries market risk, and the tech concentration, though reduced, remains a factor in potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate while maintaining significant broad exposure.
Reduce QQQI to 5% and SMH to 5% to free up capital for higher-growth opportunities.
Increase SOXL to 10%, leaning into leveraged semiconductor exposure based on scanner read.
Buy NOW at 10% and TXN at 10%, adding top-scoring, high-conviction tech setups.
Maintain a small 5% cash buffer for quick deployment or to cover margin calls.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by leaning into leverage and high-scoring growth setups. This approach significantly amplifies both potential gains and losses, with a higher risk of substantial drawdowns and daily decay from leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce growth exposure and free up capital.
Maximize QQQI to 30% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Sell all SMH, CRWD, SOXL, and SPAL to eliminate high-beta and leveraged holdings.
This rotation focuses on income-generating ETFs and broad index stability.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and reliable income.
income.read: This income plan aims for ~1% per month by rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad index. While prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is not a savings account and still carries market risk.
